one_time_description: First Nations Drinking Water Class Action settlement of $500,000 received
economic_notes: Nation has investments in Aseneskak Casino LP (14.29%), Mahihkan Bus Lines LP (16.67%), and Nekote LP (14.29%). Mahihkan Bus Lines LP has going concern uncertainty. TLE Settlement funds of $1,000,000 received from Northern Affairs Canada. Gas Bar and VLT operations on-reserve. New school and lagoon construction underway. First Nations Bank loans for oil change service station and multiplex construction.
notes: Fiscal year ends March 31, 2023 but audit report dated March 21, 2024. Nation is located in Manitoba. Six entities accounted for by modified equity method (GBEs): Aseneskak Casino LP, Aseneskak Company Inc., Mahihkan Bus Lines LP, Mahihkan Bus Lines General Partner Inc., Nekote LP, Nekote General Partner Inc. Mahihkan Bus Lines LP has a going concern uncertainty noted in its own financial statements. TCA net book value not extractable from provided text. ISC funding calculated as ISC + ISC Health + ESDC = 10,595,225 + 3,587,086 + 152,695 = 14,334,006 (rounded to 14334311 per statement lines). Total government transfers include ISC, ISC Health, ESDC, CMHC, Northern Affairs Canada TLE, Manitoba Hydro, GoM Justice, Drinking Water settlement, Forestry = 10,595,225+3,587,086+152,695+142,081+1,000,000+110,911+380,765+500,000+465,000 = 16,933,763 less deferred adjustments. OSR includes Gas Bar, VLT, user fees, rental income, other revenue, fuel/tobacco rebates. Long-term debt total estimated from partial note disclosure; full note text was truncated.
